title: Chronology-aware CatBoost with `has_time=True`
summary: Current CatBoost likely uses random permutations, but CatBoost’s ordered boosting/ordered categorical statistics were designed to reduce prediction shift from target leakage.
rationale: Current CatBoost likely uses random permutations, but CatBoost’s ordered boosting/ordered categorical statistics were designed to reduce prediction shift from target leakage. Here the row order is inherently temporal within race/stint, and rare `Driver`/`Race` categories are abundant, so preserving time order may help more than adding more ratio features.
implementation_hint: Sort train/test by `Year, Race, Driver, Stint, LapNumber, id`, set a timestamp/order column, train CatBoost with `has_time=True`, and compare against the same feature set with default permutations. Use OOF predictions as a new expert in the existing rank-blend rather than replacing the whole stack first.
expected_effect: Small but plausible gain from cleaner categorical handling and better generalization on rare drivers/races, especially if current CV is mildly optimistic.
risk: If the competition rows are not truly chronological within race contexts, forcing order can add noise; test only under forward or embargoed validation, not just shuffled group folds.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
